title: Reasoned Governance Envelope (RGE) v0.2
description: Per-turn signed evidence envelope for governed AI runtime decisions. v0.2 extends v0.1 with four optional blocks (reasoning, retrieval, subagent_chain, mcp_tool_audit) to cover MCP tool calls, RAG evidence chains, multi-agent handoffs, and reasoning-surface metadata. All additions are opt-in; v0.1 surface payloads validate against v0.2 after bumping the `schema` field.

schema.description: Schema identifier. v0.2 envelopes MUST set this exact value. v0.1 envelopes (`...v0_1`) are migrated by changing this string and (optionally) populating new v0.2 blocks.

path.description: Ordered list of pipeline block dispositions for this turn. First step is canonically `input_safety_gate`; last step is canonically `audit_layer`.

reasoning.description: Optional. Decision rationale + confidence delta + evidence links. NOT raw chain-of-thought. Provider reasoning tokens stay private; this block surfaces metadata the model publicly stated plus runtime-derived scoring. Populated when the producer surfaced a rationale or proposed an action.

retrieval.description: Optional. RAG evidence chain: admitted / cited / contradicted / rejected document IDs with per-document signed evidence. F8 implementation deferred to v0.4.1; schema reserves the block so v0.4.0 envelopes can opt-in early.

subagent_chain.description: Optional. Multi-agent handoff audit chain. Each handoff carries parent envelope hash + handoff signature. v0.1 active specification published 2026-05-25 at `examples/envelopes/subagent_chain_v0_1/` (Phionyx v0.6.0 W1).

mcp_tool_audit.description: Optional. MCP trust boundary governance evidence: tool descriptor hash, change detection, permission scope, call I/O hash, user approval, runtime anomaly flag, signed envelope reference. Reserved for v0.4.0 F1 (MCP governance recorder). status='reserved-for-v0.4.0-f1' until F1 ships.

time_delta.description: Seconds since previous turn; 0.1 for first turn.

reason.description: Optional human-readable rationale. Required when disposition is `block` or `defer`.

redacted.description: True if any block intervened on the model output (block, redact, defer).

text.description: Final text the runtime would emit. NULL when redacted.

model_proposed_action.description: Action shape proposed by the producer (e.g. tool call payload), if any.

model_stated_rationale.description: Publicly stated rationale from the model. NULL if the producer surfaced no rationale. NOT a reconstruction of private CoT.

runtime_policy_basis.description: Ordered list of policy/gate identifiers that fed the runtime decision (e.g. `input_safety_gate`, `deliberative_ethics`, `kill_switch`).

decision_reason.description: Human-readable runtime decision rationale (NOT model rationale).

rationale_action_consistency.description: Score in [0, 1]. NULL when no rationale was stated.

policy_alignment_score.description: Score in [0, 1] for alignment between stated rationale and runtime policy basis. NULL when no rationale was stated.

confidence_delta.description: Optional. Producer-reported confidence delta across the turn (e.g. pre-thinking vs post-thinking self-rated confidence). NULL when not surfaced. Provider semantics vary; see scoring_method.

ref.description: Identifier or URI for the referenced evidence.

hash.description: Optional content hash of the referenced evidence.

evidence_links.description: Optional. Pointers to evidence the rationale depends on. Reasoning is only as auditable as its evidence pointers.

scoring_method.description: Identifier of the scorer that produced rationale_action_consistency / policy_alignment_score / confidence_delta, e.g. `phionyx.governance_wrapper.v0_1.placeholder` or `phionyx.core.v0_3.reasoning_surface`.

rationale_summary.description: Optional short summary of the reasoning surface for this turn — distinct from `model_stated_rationale`. The model_stated_rationale captures whatever the producer publicly stated; rationale_summary is a runtime-produced, length-bounded one-line distillation for surfacing in dashboards and compliance reports. NULL when no rationale surface was produced. Added in v0.7.0 (F4).

kind.description: Source category. `retrieval_corpus` and `tool_output` cross-reference the existing `retrieval` and `mcp_tool_audit` blocks. `tool_descriptor_clause` references a specific clause of an MCP tool descriptor (e.g. permission scope, parameter constraint). `memory_block` references Letta/F15 memory mutations. `static_doc` covers fixed reference material loaded into context.

ref.description: Identifier or URI for the consulted source (corpus name, memory key, doc URL, etc.).

knowledge_sources_consulted.description: Optional. Ordered list of knowledge sources the producer consulted while forming this turn's reasoning. Complements `evidence_links`: evidence_links points to artefacts the rationale depends on; knowledge_sources_consulted lists what was looked at even if not ultimately cited. Added in v0.7.0 (F4).

kind.description: Constraint category. `policy` cross-references runtime_policy_basis. `user_directive` captures explicit user instructions the producer recognised. `regulatory` may point to EU AI Act Article number, NIST AI RMF subcategory, etc. `self_imposed` marks constraints the producer chose without external mandate.

ref.description: Constraint identifier (policy name, regulatory clause, user-directive text excerpt up to 200 chars, etc.).

satisfied.description: Whether the producer believes the constraint was satisfied by this turn. NULL when uncertain or non-binary.

constraints_acknowledged.description: Optional. Ordered list of constraints the producer publicly acknowledged while forming this turn's response. Surfaces constraint-awareness even when no constraint was binding for the decision. Added in v0.7.0 (F4).

retrieval.description: RAG / retrieval evidence chain. F8 implementation lands in v0.7.0 W2.2; envelopes emitted before that may still carry `status=reserved-for-v0.4.1-f8` for the reserved-block phase. New producers SHOULD emit `status=active` and populate per-document provenance.

status.description: `reserved-for-v0.4.1-f8` for envelopes emitted during the reserved phase (block exists in v0.2 schema but no populating logic). `active` once Phionyx (or any conforming producer) populates this block at runtime. v0.7.0 W2.2 (F8) flips Phionyx's own producers to `active`.

id.description: Document identifier (URI, hash, or store-specific key).

role.description: Operational role chain: retrieved → admitted → cited → contradicted → rejected → signed. Each document records its final role for this turn.

score.description: Retrieval score from the underlying store.

signed_evidence_ref.description: Optional reference to a signed evidence record (envelope or audit chain entry).

chunk_offset.description: v0.7.0 W2.2 (F8). Optional zero-indexed chunk offset within the source document when the retrieval store returns chunked content. NULL when the store returns whole documents or chunking is opaque.

source_url.description: v0.7.0 W2.2 (F8). Optional human-resolvable URL to the source document (distinct from `id`, which may be a store-internal key). NULL when no canonical URL exists (e.g. internal-only documents).

retrieved_at.description: v0.7.0 W2.2 (F8). Optional ISO-8601 UTC timestamp marking when this specific document was retrieved (may predate the envelope `timestamp_utc` when retrieval is cached). NULL when not tracked.

corpus.description: v0.7.0 W2.2 (F8). Optional corpus metadata, more semantic than `store_id`. Captures which named knowledge base + version was queried. NULL when only `store_id` is meaningful.

version.description: Optional corpus version identifier (semver, content hash, snapshot timestamp). NULL when corpus is rolling/unversioned.

language.description: Optional BCP-47 language tag of the corpus contents (e.g. `en`, `tr`, `de-DE`). NULL when multilingual or unspecified.

similarity_threshold.description: v0.7.0 W2.2 (F8). Optional block-level minimum similarity cutoff used to gate documents from `retrieved` into the candidate set. Documents with `score < similarity_threshold` were excluded before role assignment. NULL when no threshold was applied (the store returned a fixed top-k regardless of score).

query_hash.description: Optional hash of the retrieval query for replay.

query_text_hash.description: v0.7.0 W2.2 (F8). Optional SHA-256 hash of the user-or-system-formatted query TEXT (distinct from `query_hash`, which may hash the structured store-query object). Useful for replay across stores that serialize queries differently. Format: `sha256:<64-hex>`. NULL when not tracked.

status.description: v0.2-era enum. New producers (2026-05-25 onward) MUST set `active` and populate the full v0.1 surface specified in `examples/envelopes/subagent_chain_v0_1/subagent_chain_v0_1.schema.json`. `reserved-for-v0.6.0-f5` is retained for backward-compat with envelopes emitted during the reserved phase.

agent_id.description: This agent's identifier in the chain.

role.description: Position in the agent hierarchy for this turn.

parent_envelope_hash.description: Hash of the parent agent's envelope that handed off to this turn. NULL when role=`root`.

handoff_signature.description: Signed handoff payload from the parent (Ed25519 or HMAC). NULL when role=`root`.

mcp_tool_audit.description: MCP trust boundary governance evidence. 8-capability surface tracking tool descriptor integrity + per-call signed evidence + runtime anomaly detection. Implementation lands in v0.4.0 F1 (MCP governance recorder); v0.2 schema reserves the block.

status.description: `reserved-for-v0.4.0-f1` until F1 (MCP governance recorder) implementation lands. `active` once Phionyx populates this block at runtime.

tool_descriptor_hash.description: Capability 1: SHA-256 hash of the FULL tool descriptor as observed at this turn, INCLUDING the MCP `protocolVersion` field. Format: `sha256:<64-hex>`. Computed over canonical-JSON encoding (RFC 8785 / Phionyx canonical: sort_keys, no whitespace, ensure_ascii) of the complete descriptor object as received from the MCP server. Spec-version drift therefore changes the hash even when the descriptor body is identical. NULL when tool descriptor unavailable (e.g. during user-approval bootstrap before first descriptor exchange). Founder decision Q3 (2026-05-19).

descriptor_change_detected.description: Capability 2: True if the descriptor hash differs from the user-approved baseline (rug-pull detection). NULL when no baseline exists yet.

tool_permission_scope.description: Capability 3: Capability profile entries authorised for this tool at this turn (e.g. `["read_files", "http_get"]`).

tool_call_io_hash.description: Capability 4: Per-call signed evidence — content hashes of the tool input and tool output.

approval_ref.description: Optional reference to the approval audit record.

user_approval_state.description: Capability 5: Consent capture + audit. Records the user-side approval state for this tool at this turn.

source.description: Which Phionyx gate raised the flag.

runtime_anomaly_flag.description: Capability 6: Anomaly flag fed by Phionyx's behavioral_drift + action_intent_gate blocks.

signed_envelope_ref.description: Capability 7: Reference (URI or hash) to the signed RGE envelope record persisting this MCP tool audit. Typically `envelope://<integrity.current>` (self-reference). NOTE: this field is OUTSIDE the hash domain — builders + verifiers MUST normalise it to null before computing `integrity.current` to avoid a hash-fixpoint paradox. The field is persisted alongside the envelope so external consumers can resolve it, but it is NOT covered by `integrity.signature`. See RFC §4.1.

chain_verify_command.description: Capability 8: CLI command users can run to verify the chain integrity for this turn, e.g. `phionyx-mcp verify-chain --trace <trace_id> --turn <turn_index>`.

previous.description: Hash of the previous envelope in the chain. Genesis = `sha256:00000000` (8 zero bytes truncated).

current.description: Hash of this envelope's payload + previous. SHA-256 over canonical JSON, truncated to 16 hex chars in the demo wrapper; full digest in Core.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature in Core (`ed25519:<hex>`) or `demo-hmac:<hex>` in the launch wrapper. Signing key identity tracked separately.

canonical_json.description: True if the hash was computed over canonical-JSON-encoded payload (sort_keys, no whitespace, ASCII-safe).
